executive_summary: Your supply chain faces two distinct urgency tiers. **Immediate:** Stora Enso (Finland, printing substrates) and Infineon (Germany, EE components) both posted severe unexplained stock crashes (-8.5% and -6.2% respectively) with critical risk flags—investigate root causes within 48 hours as these underpin core production. Texas Instruments (USA, EE components) is trending down (-4.4%) with high BAT exposure and warrants close monitoring. **Secondary but systemic:** Five China-based suppliers (Huizhou BYD, Smoore, EVE Energy, Porton, IP Sun) plus Tae Young Filters (South Korea) are all flagged for identical geopolitical pressures (US-China trade war, Taiwan risk, export controls), creating correlated exposure across EMS, batteries, and nicotine categories—diversification away from this cluster should begin immediately. Sappi (South Africa) and ITC (India) carry medium regional risks (energy/infrastructure and border tensions) but are less time-critical. Focus your next 72 hours on the unexplained stock crashes and China exposure mapping; geopolitical risks are slower-moving but wider-reaching.
